query: What did Bahá'u'lláh teach about the oneness of humanity?
response: Bahá'u'lláh's teachings on the oneness of humanity are profound and central to His message. He elevates "the unity of mankind" above all other aspirations, emphasizing its importance in countless writings ([*Tablet to Mánikchí Sáhib*](https://siftersearch.com/document/16692)). This principle is not just a lofty ideal but a practical goal, as Shoghi Effendi describes it as the "World Unity the Goal" of Bahá'u'lláh's vision ([*The World Order of Bahá’u’lláh*](https://siftersearch.com/document/16277)). The teachings call for a transformation in how we see each other, urging us to "sanctify your souls" and detach from worldly divisions to truly understand and embrace this unity ([*The Kitáb-i-Íqán*](https://siftersearch.com/document/8300)).

query: Find passages about love in the Bahá'í writings
response: Bahá'u'lláh beautifully describes love as "the Beloved’s splendour in the world," highlighting its divine nature and presence in our lives ([*A Hymn to Love*](https://siftersearch.com/document/11447)). He also uses the metaphor of fragrance, saying love sheds "the Beloved’s fragrance upon the soul," symbolizing the bounties and beauty of God that love brings to us ([*A Hymn to Love*](https://siftersearch.com/document/11447)). Furthermore, love is described as "the Companion of the soul," suggesting that it is an intimate friend and comforter, always present to solace and support us ([*A Hymn to Love*](https://siftersearch.com/document/11447)). These passages reflect the profound and multifaceted nature of love in the Bahá'í writings.

query: What does Bahá'u'lláh say about justice, and where can I read more?
response: Bahá'u'lláh places great emphasis on justice, describing it as "the best beloved of all things" in His sight ([*The Hidden Words*](https://siftersearch.com/document/8230)). He encourages us to "turn not away therefrom" and to see and know "with thine own eyes" rather than relying on others' perceptions ([*The Hidden Words*](https://siftersearch.com/document/8230)). Justice is both a divine and human attribute, a "gift" and a "sign of My loving-kindness" that allows us to rule ourselves and others with "pure justice" ([*Paradise of Justice*](https://siftersearch.com/document/11446)). If you're interested in exploring these teachings further, you might want to read Bahá'u'lláh's *Paradise of Justice* and *The Hidden Words*.
